framework.name: OAuth Has No Delegation Chain Concept
framework.description: Microsoft's Alex Simons confirms agents need 'their own defined set of privileges — not just proxy a user's rights.' OAuth treats tokens as impersonation rather than delegated authority, with no mechanism for distinguishing whether an agent acts for a user, itself, or through a chain of agents.
framework.raw_quote: agents need 'their own defined set of privileges - not just proxy a user's rights'. Actions must be traceable, distinguishing if an agent is acting for a user, itself, or through a chain of agents.
